description:
Fixes the lineage principle (failed 6/6 in the jarvis adherence review): no artifact records the code version that produced it. Automatically capture into every run record and artifact lineage entry: git SHA + dirty flag, argv/command line, config snapshot (or content hash), timestamp, upstream artifact ids. Zero API change — benefits even repos that only use the monitor. Smallest, do first.

## Acceptance criteria
Any run started via stagehand writes a manifest.json (or equivalent record) containing git SHA, dirty flag, argv, config snapshot, upstream artifact ids; artifacts created via the artifacts service carry a pointer back to the producing manifest; works with no user code changes beyond existing stagehand usage.

## Notes
Implemented on branch run-manifest → https://github.com/dtch1997/stagehand/pull/19 (v1.5.0, 111 tests pass). Close on merge.
title: Auto run-manifest: stamp git SHA + config + upstream ids on every run/artifact
